name: Barranco de la Hoz y Virgen de la Hoz — circular by the Parque de Molina de Aragón-Alto Tajo
name_original: Barranco de la Hoz y Virgen de la Hoz — circular por el Parque de Molina de Aragón-Alto Tajo
region_intro: Hiking around Corduente offers access to the Alto Tajo Natural Park, situated at an elevation of 1051 meters above sea level. The region is characterized by crags, dehesa, and extensive pine forests, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. The landscape features dramatic gorges carved by rivers, such as the Barranco de la Hoz, with unique sandstone formations and Mediterranean forests. The Gallo River flows through the area, contributing to its natural beauty and shaping the canyon's reddish walls. Best hiking trails around Corduente The most popular hiking route is Barranco de la Hoz y Virgen de la Hoz — circular by the Parque de Molina de Aragón-Alto Tajo, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 2 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the iconic Barranco de la Hoz gorge, showcasing unique rock formations and leading to the Santuario de la Virgen de la Hoz. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Rodenal del Corduente – circular desde Corduente – Parque Natural del Alto Tajo, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This circular trail starts from Corduente and often follows paths alongside the Gallo River, offering views of the surrounding natural park. Local hikers also love the Beach of Puente de San Pedro and waterfall of Campillo - circular in the Natural Park of Alto Tajo, a 5.4 miles (8.8 km) trail leading through the Alto Tajo Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. This route features river sections and a waterfall. Hiking around Corduente is defined by dramatic river gorges, unique rock formations, and extensive pine forests within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Corduente are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 240 hikers have used komoot to explore Corduente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails are available around Corduente?
A: Corduente offers a variety of hiking trails, primarily within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. You'll find routes ranging from easy to moderate difficulty, with options that explore dramatic river gorges, unique rock formations, and extensive pine forests. There are nearly 20 routes available, with 3 easy and 14 moderate trails.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Corduente?
A: Yes, Corduente is an excellent starting point for circular hikes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1121724970/el-rodenal-del-corduente-circular-desde-corduente-parque-natural-del-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rodenal del Corduente – circular desde Corduente – Parque Natural del Alto Tajo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 12.1 km path that often follows the Gallo River. Another highly-rated circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030089334/barranco-de-la-hoz-y-virgen-de-la-hoz-circular-by-the-parque-de-molina-de-aragon-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Hoz y Virgen de la Hoz — circular by the Parque de Molina de Aragón-Alto Tajo&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the iconic gorge.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on a hike near Corduente?
A: Hiking around Corduente offers stunning natural features. The most iconic is the &lt;strong&gt;Barranco de la Hoz&lt;/strong&gt;, a gorge carved by the Gallo River, featuring unique rock formations like "La Tinaja" and "El Huso." You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6385115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Way of the Cross&lt;/a&gt; and experience the dramatic landscape of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful stretch between the cliffs&lt;/a&gt; along the Tajo River.

Q: Are there any waterfalls along the hiking trails in Corduente?
A: Yes, you can find waterfalls on some routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027382382/beach-of-puente-de-san-pedro-and-waterfall-of-campillo-circular-in-the-natural-park-of-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of Puente de San Pedro and waterfall of Campillo - circular in the Natural Park of Alto Tajo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.8 km trail that specifically features a waterfall, making it a great option for those looking for this natural attraction.

Q: What historical sites or attractions are near the hiking routes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Corduente offers historical interest. The &lt;strong&gt;Santuario de la Virgen de la Hoz&lt;/strong&gt; is uniquely nestled within the Barranco de la Hoz and is accessible via hiking trails. Additionally, the 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Molina de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5457035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilnuevo Castle&lt;/a&gt; are nearby, offering historical context and scenic views.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Corduente?
A: The hiking trails around Corduente are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic river gorges, unique rock formations, and the tranquil atmosphere of the Alto Tajo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Corduente offers 3 easy hiking trails, perfect for beginners or families looking for a less strenuous experience. These routes allow you to enjoy the natural beauty of the Alto Tajo Natural Park without significant elevation gain or long distances.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Corduente?
A: Corduente, situated at 1051 meters above sea level, offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, but the pine forests and river areas provide shade. Winter hiking is possible, but it's advisable to check local weather conditions as the elevation can bring colder temperatures.

Q: Can I find trails that follow the Tajo River?
A: Absolutely. The region is shaped by the Tajo River, and several trails offer opportunities to walk along its banks. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027568283/along-the-banks-of-the-tajo-river-to-the-vado-de-salmeron-recreational-area-alto-tajo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Along the banks of the Tajo River to the Vado de Salmeron recreational area — Alto Tajo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12.6 km trail that specifically follows the river, offering beautiful riverside views.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic overlooks on the trails?
A: Yes, the dramatic landscape of Corduente provides several scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1114022858/mirador-de-tajo-zaorejas-circular-desde-puente-de-san-pedro-parque-natural-del-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Tajo Zaorejas – circular desde Puente De San Pedro – Parque Natural del Alto Tajo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.4 km route specifically designed to lead to a viewpoint overlooking the Tajo River, offering expansive vistas of the natural park.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Corduente?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not listed, Corduente is a small village and a common starting point for many routes. It's generally possible to find parking within or on the outskirts of the village, especially for popular circular routes that begin and end there.
tips:
The Barranco de la Hoz, also known as Barranco de la Virgen de la Hoz, is a magical enclave in the province of Guadalajara, within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. This place, carved over the centuries by the Gallo River, presents a spectacular reddish canyon, one of the most beautiful in the Iberian Peninsula and the most visited in the Alto Tajo region.

Here, legends and nature intermingle to create a landscape with a unique magic. Barranco de la Hoz is located just 15 kilometers from Molina de Aragón, between Corduente and Ventosa.

Hospedería Virgen de la Hoz is a charming rural accommodation. This place offers a peaceful and natural setting, ideal for relaxing and enjoying outdoor activities such as hiking, cycling and bird watching.
The inn has cosy and well-equipped rooms, a restaurant serving delicious à la carte dishes, and a terrace for relaxing. In addition, guests can enjoy Wi-Fi in public areas.
